name: Konaki-jiji
description: Konaki-jiji, meaning 'crying old man baby,' is a yokai from Japanese folklore. This creature appears as an infant, often abandoned near mountain paths or in remote areas. However, despite its seemingly helpless appearance, Konaki-jiji possesses incredible strength. Its primary method of attack involves luring unsuspecting travelers to pick it up, after which it will cling tightly and increase its weight to an unbearable degree, crushing or severely injuring the victim. Some tales suggest Konaki-jiji is a shapeshifting henge, capable of altering its appearance to further deceive its targets. The creature is often associated with mountainous regions and is considered a mischievous, and sometimes dangerous, trickster spirit. While not inherently evil, its actions can have devastating consequences for those who fall prey to its deceptive tactics. The legends surrounding Konaki-jiji serve as cautionary tales about the dangers of trusting appearances and the unpredictable nature of the spirit world.
subtitle: The Crying Old Man Baby Yokai
appearance: Konaki-jiji appears as a seemingly normal human infant, often swaddled in traditional baby clothing. However, its face is that of a wizened old man, with wrinkled skin and a mournful expression. Its eyes are often described as being beady and filled with a deceptive sadness. While appearing small and fragile, its grip is incredibly strong, and its body can become immensely heavy.
habitat: Mountainous regions, remote paths, abandoned areas, near forests
behavior: Konaki-jiji is primarily a trickster spirit. It uses its cries to attract the attention of passersby, feigning distress to lure them into picking it up. Once held, it clings tightly and increases its weight dramatically, often crushing its victims. It is generally solitary and mischievous, rather than overtly malevolent, but its actions can be harmful.
weaknesses: Exposure (some tales suggest sunlight weakens it)

keyMyths.title: The Crushing Weight
keyMyths.description: A traveler hears a baby crying in the woods and picks it up to help. The baby, Konaki-jiji, suddenly becomes incredibly heavy, crushing the traveler beneath its weight. The traveler is left severely injured, learning a harsh lesson about trusting appearances.

sources.author: Shigeru Mizuki
sources.description: Mizuki's manga and anime series feature Konaki-jiji, popularizing the yokai and its abilities.
sources.text: Mizuki's manga and anime series feature Konaki-jiji, popularizing the yokai and its abilities.

sources.author: Kazuhiko Komatsu
sources.description: A comprehensive guide to various yokai, including Konaki-jiji, detailing its origins and characteristics.
